message:
Improve CMake preset documentation and fix preset names

Reorganize build-from-source to emphasize CMake presets as the
recommended quick-start approach, with manual configuration as an
alternative. Fix preset name typos (redeb → relwithdebinfo) and add
examples showing how to list and use presets. Update
build-environment.mdx with the same preset name corrections and usage
examples.

files.patch:
@@ -32,15 +32,28 @@ use_flake2

 The `tenzir/tenzir` repository comes with a set of CMake configure and build
-presets that can be used in this environment:
+presets that can be used in this environment. List available presets with:
+
+```sh
+cmake --list-presets
+```
+
+Common presets for development:
 
 - `nix-clang-debug`
-- `nix-clang-redeb`
+- `nix-clang-relwithdebinfo`
 - `nix-clang-release`
 - `nix-gcc-debug`
-- `nix-gcc-redeb`
+- `nix-gcc-relwithdebinfo`
 - `nix-gcc-release`
 
+Configure and build using a preset:
+
+```sh
+cmake --preset nix-clang-debug
+cmake --build --preset nix-clang-debug
+```
+
 :::note
 This build environment is currently only tested on Linux.
 :::

-Configure the build with CMake. For faster builds, we recommend passing
-`-G Ninja` to `cmake`.
+### Quick start with presets
+
+The repository includes CMake presets that combine configuration options into
+curated build flavors. Start by discovering the available presets:
+
+```sh
+cmake --list-presets
+```
+
+This shows presets for different compilers (Clang, GCC), build types (`Debug`,
+`Release`, `RelWithDebInfo`), and environments (Nix, macOS). Pick a preset matching
+your setup and configure the build:
+
+```sh
+cmake --preset <preset-name>
+```
+
+For example, to set up a release build on macOS:
+
+```sh
+cmake --preset macos-clang-release
+cmake --build --preset macos-clang-release
+```
+
+### Manual configuration
+
+For custom configurations without presets, invoke CMake directly:
 
 ```sh
 cmake -B build
 # CMake defaults to a "Debug" build. When performance matters, use "Release"
 cmake -B build -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release
 ```
 
+For faster builds, we recommend passing `-G Ninja` to `cmake`.
+
 Optionally, you can use the CMake TUI to visually configure the build:
 
 ```sh
 ccmake build
 ```
